name: Hikma Health Inc
mission: We partner with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations. We create customized data systems for these healthc...
theoryOfChange: Hikma Health partners with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations to create customized data systems and offer clinicians data-driven tools. By making healthcare data accessible, secure, and actionable (including enabling patients to access/port their records and enabling clinicians to access authorized patient records across clinics), they aim to improve patient outcomes and support personalized, continuous care.

grantsData.purpose: Grant to Parkers Mobile Clinic, a non profit in Nigeria providing healthcare services to deploy the Hikma Health EHR system.

scoreSummary: HIKMA HEALTH INC shows strong alignment and good impact (minor risk deductions applied).

dimension_explanations.alignment: Hikma Health addresses high-urgency global health needs through technological innovation [1]. While it offers sadaqah-eligible giving opportunities for donors interested in healthcare infrastructure, it maintains a medium alignment for specific Muslim donor segments due to its general humanitarian focus [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building by creating the Health and Environmental Record System (HERS) and managing deployments in Latin America [1]. Its financial health is robust, characterized by a 100.0% program expense ratio and 26.0 months of working capital, which supports long-term operational stability [2].

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that digital health tools actually improve patient outcomes rather than just adding administrative burden.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="3">revenue of only $70,494</cite>, the organization has significant room to grow its technical team and expand to new regions.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">1.0% of its expenses toward program services</cite>, ensuring that every dollar spent goes toward healthcare technology deployment.
Hikma Health spends <cite id="6">$0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, a rare metric that indicates zero direct fundraising overhead.
The charity maintains <cite id="7">26.0 months of working capital</cite>, which is significantly higher than the industry standard for small nonprofits.

strengths_deep_dive: Hikma Health's technical capacity is its primary differentiator, offering an <cite id="2">offline-ready EHR system</cite> specifically designed for the challenges of refugee camps and rural clinics. Their commitment to transparency is reflected in their <cite id="9">Silver Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates a <cite id="10">documented theory of change</cite>, indicating a strategic approach to how their data systems lead to improved health outcomes for the <cite id="11">underserved populations they serve in Nicaragua</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Hikma Health Inc. is a specialized global health technology nonprofit that <cite id="1">partners with organizations to provide data systems</cite> for refugee and underserved populations. The organization focuses on creating customized, <cite id="2">offline-ready Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems</cite> and health information infrastructure to improve clinical outcomes in resource-constrained environments like Nicaragua. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">$70,494 in total revenue</cite>, a significant decrease from previous years as evidenced by a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of -37.1%</cite>. Despite this smaller scale, Hikma Health maintains a highly lean financial profile, reporting a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 100.0%</cite>, meaning all reported spending is directed toward its core mission of health system deployment. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the organization does not currently incur direct costs to solicit public donations. While the organization operates with a modest budget, it maintains <cite id="7">strong liquidity with 26.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant runway for its ongoing technical projects and <cite id="8">Care Innovation Grant</cite> initiatives.
